install_tell: docker run -d -p 3210:3210 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxxx --name lobe-chat lobehub/lobe-chat   # client-side quickstart; ACCESS_CODE optional. Database edition: mkdir lobe-chat-db && cd lobe-chat-db && bash <(curl -fsSL https://lobe.li/setup.sh) && docker compose up -d
misconfig_patterns: ACCESS_CODE left unset -> fully open UI+API exposed to the internet on :3210 (the dominant misconfig)
Operator sets OPENAI_API_KEY server-side but no ACCESS_CODE -> anyone reaching the host proxies LLM calls on the operator's billing (LLMjacking)
Running an unpatched client-side image (<=2.1.56) reachable from the internet -> unauth /webapi/proxy SSRF live
Database edition deployed but reverse-proxy/ingress exposes :3210 directly, bypassing the intended auth fronting
KEY_VAULTS_SECRET committed or weak -> DB-stored user API keys decryptable if the Postgres store is also exposed

response_markers: POST /webapi/proxy with a URL body returns a server-side fetched response (pre-2.1.57, unauth)
GET / served by Next.js with _next/static chunks and inline self.__next_f bootstrap referencing lobe-chat
active_probe.false_positive_check: "chat", "AI", and "LobeChat" branding collide with the hundreds of forks/derivatives and the AIDotNet/lobe-chat .NET reskin. To avoid the ~50% false positive: (1) confirm port 3210 AND the Next.js app shell, (2) require the vendor-unique /webapi/ route family (no other app namespaces chat under /webapi/chat/[provider]) or the XOR-obfuscated X-lobe-chat-auth request header — this header string is unique to LobeHub's runtime, (3) /manifest.webmanifest must return short_name "LobeChat". A bare "LobeChat" title with no /webapi/ surface is likely a static clone or a reverse-proxied marketing page, not a live instance. Distinguish editions: client-side image is fully open at "/"; database edition redirects "/" to a Better-Auth/Casdoor login — the presence of /api/auth (Better-Auth) vs only ACCESS_CODE gating tells the edition apart.

deployment_tells: Port 3210 (default; classic client-side image binds container :3210)
Next.js app shell: _next/static chunks, self.__next_f bootstrap, /manifest.webmanifest served at root
X-lobe-chat-auth request header on chat calls (XOR-obfuscated payload)
Two editions: lobehub/lobe-chat (client-side, ACCESS_CODE, default-off) vs lobehub/lobe-chat-database (Postgres+pgvector+MinIO+Casdoor, Better-Auth)
setup.sh bootstrap from lobe.li/setup.sh + docker compose up -d for the database edition
Casdoor login screen (database edition) or bare chat UI with no login (client-side, open)
pivot_paths: GET / — open client-side instance loads straight into the chat UI with no login; confirms unauth read access without sending any chat
GET /manifest.webmanifest — confirm LobeChat identity (read-only, no auth)
POST /webapi/proxy {url:...} on <=2.1.56 — unauth SSRF to enumerate cloud metadata / internal services (read-only enumeration; do not exfiltrate)
Forge X-lobe-chat-auth on <=2.1.47 to read /webapi/models/[provider] and confirm which upstream providers are configured without spending tokens
Knowledge-base UI (open instance) — enumerate ingested document names to gauge data sensitivity (names ARE the finding; do not download)
vulnerabilities: GHSA-5mwj-v5jw-5c97 (Moderate) — Authentication bypass via forgeable X-lobe-chat-auth header (only XOR-obfuscated with a hardcoded repo key, not signed); unauth attacker forges {"apiKey":"x"} to reach /webapi/chat|models|create-image routes and spend server credentials; affects @lobehub/lobehub <= 2.1.47, patched 2.1.48

GHSA-xmwj-c75x-6346 (Moderate) — Unauthenticated SSRF in POST /webapi/proxy; route lacks checkAuth() so any user makes arbitrary server-side outbound requests, leak infra/Vercel details, inject Set-Cookie on lobehub.com; affects <= v2.1.56, patched 2.1.57

CVE-2024-47066 / GHSA-3fc8-2r3f-8wrg (Critical) — Insufficient fix for CVE-2024-32964; SSRF protection bypassable via HTTP redirect to internal resources on /api/proxy, unauthenticated; affects <= v1.19.12, patched 1.19.13
GHSA-4gpc-rhpj-9443 (Critical) — Stored XSS in chat messages escalating to RCE
GHSA-m79r-r765-5f9j (Moderate) — RCE via XSS in desktop chat messages
GHSA-wrrr-8jcv-wjf5 (Moderate) — Authorization flaw in presigned upload with user-controlled object keys
GHSA-j7xp-4mg9-x28r (Low) — IDOR in knowledge-base file removal allows cross-user file deletion
GHSA-fgx4-p8xf-qhp9 (Low) — SSRF via native web-fetch module
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE — env-var reference (docs/self-hosting/environment-variables/basic.mdx, mirrored at deepwiki 7.1): ACCESS_CODE is described as "Password to lock the application. Supports comma-separated values" with default value shown as "-" (unset/empty). When ACCESS_CODE is empty/unset the instance is accessible without authentication. The classic client-side quickstart docker run command does NOT include it: `docker run -d -p 3210:3210 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxxx --name lobe-chat lobehub/lobe-chat` yields a fully unauth UI+API; ACCESS_CODE is only added as an OPTIONAL `-e ACCESS_CODE=lobe66` flag in the "add a password" example. The repo .env.example ships no ACCESS_CODE and no admin password. Conclusion: auth exists (ACCESS_CODE / Better-Auth) but is OFF in the default client-side deployment unless an env var is set. The newer database edition (lobehub/lobe-chat-database) instead requires DATABASE_URL + AUTH_SECRET + KEY_VAULTS_SECRET and front-ends with Better-Auth/Casdoor, but the canonical single-container quickstart most operators run is the client-side image, which is default-off.
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini/Bedrock/Azure) — in client-side mode keys configured via ACCESS_CODE-gated UI proxy through /webapi/chat/[provider]; an open instance lets anyone spend them (LLMjacking)
Ingested RAG knowledge-base documents and their parsed chunks/embeddings (the file knowledge base feature) — potential PII/business docs
Conversation/session history and assistant (agent) definitions revealing prompts and business logic
In database edition: PostgreSQL/pgvector store contents and user-provided API keys encrypted with KEY_VAULTS_SECRET (exposure of secret + DB = key recovery)
MinIO/S3 object storage holding uploaded files and KB sources
Server-side fetch capability via /webapi/proxy usable as an SSRF pivot into cloud metadata / internal services
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
